name: Code integrity determined image hash is not valid
definition: Windows Security Event ID 5038 records Code integrity determined image hash is not valid.
trigger_scenarios: Windows logs the event when the audited security operation occurs.

key_fields.explanation: Image Name under C:\\Users\\ or C:\\Temp\\ with an invalid hash is untrusted executable loading; compare the expected catalog hash and signer.
The initiating account and Logon ID support attribution and correlation to 4624 and 4688.
The affected host determines the operational impact.

false_positives: Documented maintenance, policy changes, or approved administration can generate this event.
Validate change control and host role before suppressing.
detection_notes: Image Name under C:\\Users\\ or C:\\Temp\\ with an invalid hash is untrusted executable loading; compare the expected catalog hash and signer. This is a T1553.002 Subvert Trust Controls: Code Signing signal; preserve the concrete value and correlate the actor, process, and related events.
kql_snippet:
SecurityEvent
| where EventID == 5038
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account
